summary: Clear soundboard capture of a legendary New Speedway Boogie breakout backed by strong second‑set jams and stellar Black Peter.
review: The recording is a solid soundboard mix that’s generally clear, though a few reviewers note the keyboards and Bob/Weir guitars sit too high in the blend. Musically the show is celebrated for the surprise “New Speedway Boogie” breakout, an inspired Black Peter, and an extended China‑Rider‑Fingers jam that make it one of the standout 1991 performances.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews praise the performance (especially New Speedway Boogie and Black Peter) while noting only moderate mix issues; this yields a strong show rating with good‑quality soundboard audio.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Most reviewers describe the download as clear and crisp, but several call out overly loud keyboards (Vince) and guitars (Bob/Weir) that sometimes mask Jerry’s lead.

show_quality.setlist_flow: A strong first set opens with a punchy Bertha and builds to the surprise New Speedway Boogie breakout, while the second set delivers deep jams (China‑Rider‑Fingers) and soulful ballads like Black Peter.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for ripping on New Speedway Boogie and delivering a 'whaling' solo on Black Peter; occasional mix issues hide his guitar.
band_member_comments.Bob: His rhythm guitar is noted as both comically loud in the mix and, by some, a powerful part of the show’s energy.
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince's keyboards are frequently cited as mixed too high, stepping on Jerry, yet also recognized for defining the modern 1990s sound.
